steps.title: Find the dangling modifier
steps.content: The opening phrase "Having 11,486 feet of prominence" describes whatever follows the comma. It must describe the mountain, not the geographers.

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
The introductory phrase "Having 11,486 feet of prominence" must describe the grammatical subject that follows the comma — only a mountain can have that prominence.
  • Choice A: "Having 11,486 feet of prominence, geographers have ranked Mount Hayes..." — "geographers" is the subject; geographers don't have 11,486 feet of prominence. Dangling modifier. ✗
  • Choice B: "Having 11,486 feet of prominence, Mount Hayes, a peak located in the United States, is ranked..." — "Mount Hayes" is the subject; the mountain has the prominence. ✓
  • Choice C: "Having 11,486 feet of prominence, the list of mountains...includes Mount Hayes..." — "the list" is the subject; lists don't have topographical prominence. Dangling modifier. ✗
  • Choice D: "Having 11,486 feet of prominence, the ranking given by geographers to Mount Hayes..." — "the ranking" is the subject; rankings don't have prominence. Dangling modifier. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
